authors_affiliation: Tomas Vicar, Jakub Hejc, Petra Novotna, Marina Ronzhina, Oto Janousek; Department of Biomedical Engineering, Brno University of Technology, Brno, Czech Republic

result.evaluation_split: official hidden test set (full test set, out of 40 teams in the official ranking); challenge validation score was 0.696

self_reported_metrics.other: Dice Coefficient (DC): 0.571 on training hold-out subset, 0.522 on validation data, 0.277 on full test set. Challenge Metric (CM) per hidden database: Database 1 = 0.892 (same-source data, best of 40 teams), Database 2 = 0.235, Database 3 = 0.104 (different-source data).

model.architecture_details: 1D ResNet-style CNN feature extractor with 6 residual blocks (3 convolutional layers each, kernel size 3), local skip connections plus an additional global skip connection from the input layer, followed by Global Max Pooling and a final classification layer of 24 independent fully-connected binary classifiers (termed 'BUTT' - Binary Units Training Technique) with sigmoid outputs.
model.key_novelty: Extends a 1D ResNet with a global skip connection to the input, and derives a custom differentiable loss function directly from the official Challenge weighted-confusion-matrix metric, combined with per-class Bayesian-optimized decision thresholds.

input.signal_length_window: variable length; zero-padded to equal length for mini-batch training, with zero-padded portion cropped out immediately after the ResNet layers (before pooling) to avoid distorting pooled features; long Holter recordings split into segments at inference
input.sampling_rate: resampled to 125 Hz (source data at 257, 500, or 1000 Hz)
input.preprocessing: Time-domain resampling to a fixed 125 Hz via linear interpolation combined with decimation and an anti-aliasing FIR filter; zero-padding for batch formation.

data.training_datasets: 43,101 labeled recordings from the 6 official Challenge training databases; no external data reported
data.class_imbalance_handling: Weighted cross-entropy (WCE) loss with per-class positive/negative weights inversely proportional to class frequency
data.augmentation_techniques: Random ±10% stretch along the temporal axis (p=0.8) and ±20% amplification along the voltage axis (p=0.8); other tested preprocessing augmentations (baseline drift removal, temporal shifting, noise addition, simulated electrode switch) had no measurable effect.

training.loss_function: Two-phase training: weighted cross-entropy (WCE) in the first learning-rate cycle, then a custom differentiable 'CM Loss' derived from the official Challenge metric's weighted confusion matrix in the second cycle
training.optimizer: Adam (β1=0.9, β2=0.999) with decoupled weight decay (λ=1e-5), initial learning rate 1e-3, two-cycle schedule decaying by 0.1x every 30 epochs per cycle, mini-batch size 32; Stochastic Weight Averaging applied across epochs
training.cross_validation_strategy: 10% random hold-out subset of training data for internal validation (no k-fold CV reported); final model is a majority-vote ensemble of 3 bootstrap-aggregated models each fit on a 90% subset of training data

contribution.summary: Proposes a 1D ResNet with local and global skip connections and per-class binary classification heads ('BUTT'), trained using a custom loss function derived directly from the official Challenge metric plus Bayesian-optimized class-specific thresholds, but shows this approach generalizes poorly to ECG data from sources unseen in training.
